sourceLabel: Lafayette Consolidated Government FY 2025-2026 Adopted Operating Budget
contextNote: Lafayette runs as a consolidated city-parish government with its own publicly-owned utility (LUS — electric, water, sewer, telecom). The $803.7M total spans services Canadian municipalities don't deliver; the city general-fund subset (~$260M) would compare more cleanly but the line-item PDF is too large to extract via automated tooling.
dataStatusNote: Lafayette is governed by the Lafayette Consolidated Government (LCG) — a unified city-parish structure with no Canadian equivalent. The full LCG operating budget for FY2025-26 is $803.7M, but that includes parish-wide funds (sheriff, courts, parish library, drainage districts), the Lafayette Utilities System (LUS — electric/water/sewer/telecom), and special revenue funds that fall outside Canadian municipal scope. Normalized total ($260M, est.) reflects the City of Lafayette General Fund only — police, fire, public works, parks/rec, planning, general government, debt — for like-for-like comparison. Confirmed within the normalized scope: police + fire combined ≈ $81M; road and bridge investment ≈ $31M+. The remaining buckets are proportional estimates pending line-item transcription from the official 2025-2026 Adopted Budget Book PDF.

excludedCategories.name: Lafayette Utilities System (LUS — electric, water, sewer, telecom)
Parish-wide funds (sheriff, courts, parish library, registrar, jail)
Drainage districts, special revenue funds, capital projects (non-operating)

normalization.note: Excluded so percentages reflect a Canadian-comparable municipal scope. The 'totalOperating' field shows the un-normalized LCG total ($803.7M) for transparency; bucket shares are computed against 'totalOperatingNormalized' (the City of Lafayette General Fund, ~$260M est.). Excluded-category amounts are approximate and should be refined from the official budget book.

nativeDepartments.name: Lafayette Police Department + Lafayette Fire Department (combined ≈ $81M, confirmed)
Public Works (roads, streets, traffic) — $31M+ in road/bridge investment confirmed; total estimated
Funded by Lafayette Utilities System (LUS) — separate utility revenue, excluded from the general fund comparison
Mayor-President's office, Administration, HR, IT, Legal, Clerk (estimated)
Debt service, capital from operating, reserves (estimated)
Grants to outside agencies, contingency, drainage (within general fund) (estimated)
